<findings>

[suggestion] [info] Zone "crash" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [info] Zone "crash" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating packages/web/src/viewer/components/crash/ and moving related files there
[suggestion] [info] Move packages/web/tests/helpers/crash-detector-test-support.ts into packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ alongside crash-detector.test.ts to eliminate the singleton helpers/ directory. The file has exactly one consumer (crash-detector.test.ts), so there is no sharing benefit from a separate directory.
[observation] [info] Cohesion (0.6) and coupling (0.4) are both within healthy bounds — this is a well-encapsulated satellite zone.
[observation] [info] Per CLAUDE.md governance, crash sits at the dual-fragility boundary (cohesion 0.5 threshold). Any new file addition should be reviewed against the two-consumer rule before it is merged.
[observation] [info] The barrel export (crash/index.ts) combined with co-located tests follows the expected satellite-zone pattern; maintain the barrel discipline as the zone grows.
[pattern] [info] crash-detector.ts intentionally bypasses external.ts to import ViewId directly from shared/ — this is a documented cycle-prevention exemption validated by boundary-check.test.ts. Other zones needing to avoid bidirectional cycles should follow this same direct-shared-import pattern rather than routing through the viewer gateway.
[relationship] [info] The crash→web-viewer bidirectional edge is driven entirely by the hook/component layer (use-crash-recovery.ts, crash-recovery-banner.ts), not by crash-detector.ts. Isolating the hook-layer coupling from the core detection logic preserves independent testability of the detection logic and prevents the bidirectional edge from growing further.
[anti-pattern] [warning] crash/index.ts barrel imports crash-recovery-banner.ts from a parent/sibling directory (components/) rather than from within the crash/ subdirectory, inverting the normal barrel-enclosure pattern. This makes crash/ the only zone barrel in the web package that reaches upward — moving crash-recovery-banner.ts into the crash/ subdirectory would complete the consolidation already recommended by existing zone insights and restore the conventional barrel direction.

</findings>

<insights>

- Zone is well-contained with co-located unit tests and a clean barrel export via crash/index.ts.
- Sits at the dual-fragility threshold boundary per CLAUDE.md — proactively apply the two-consumer rule before cohesion degrades.
- The 2 inbound imports from web-viewer are the only external coupling, keeping the blast radius small.
- Zone "crash" has files across 5 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- Zone "crash" was identified from filename patterns, not directory structure — consider creating packages/web/src/viewer/components/crash/ and moving related files there
- Cohesion (0.6) and coupling (0.4) are both within healthy bounds — this is a well-encapsulated satellite zone.
- The barrel export (crash/index.ts) combined with co-located tests follows the expected satellite-zone pattern; maintain the barrel discipline as the zone grows.
- Per CLAUDE.md governance, crash sits at the dual-fragility boundary (cohesion 0.5 threshold). Any new file addition should be reviewed against the two-consumer rule before it is merged.
- crash-detector.ts is intentionally zero-framework: it imports only from shared/view-id.ts directly (not via external.ts), explicitly bypassing the gateway pattern to prevent a bidirectional cycle. This direct-shared-import-as-cycle-prevention pattern is documented in boundary-check.test.ts and should serve as the reference model for other zones facing the same trade-off.
- The bidirectional crash ↔ web-viewer coupling is architecturally asymmetric: crash-detector.ts is fully decoupled from web-viewer, while the hook layer (use-crash-recovery.ts) drives the crash→web-viewer edge. This means the core detection logic is independently testable — the cycle exists only at the hook/component integration layer.
- crash-detector.ts intentionally bypasses external.ts to import ViewId directly from shared/ — this is a documented cycle-prevention exemption validated by boundary-check.test.ts. Other zones needing to avoid bidirectional cycles should follow this same direct-shared-import pattern rather than routing through the viewer gateway.
- The crash→web-viewer bidirectional edge is driven entirely by the hook/component layer (use-crash-recovery.ts, crash-recovery-banner.ts), not by crash-detector.ts. Isolating the hook-layer coupling from the core detection logic preserves independent testability of the detection logic and prevents the bidirectional edge from growing further.
- crash-recovery-banner.ts lives in components/ while crash/index.ts barrel is in crash/ — the barrel must import from a sibling/parent directory (../components/crash-recovery-banner.ts rather than from within its own subtree). This inverts the normal barrel-enclosure pattern and is the only case in the web package where a zone barrel reaches upward into a parent directory — a latent confusion point as the zone grows.
- crash/index.ts barrel imports crash-recovery-banner.ts from a parent/sibling directory (components/) rather than from within the crash/ subdirectory, inverting the normal barrel-enclosure pattern. This makes crash/ the only zone barrel in the web package that reaches upward — moving crash-recovery-banner.ts into the crash/ subdirectory would complete the consolidation already recommended by existing zone insights and restore the conventional barrel direction.
- tests/helpers/ is a singleton directory created solely for crash-detector-test-support.ts — the only file in that directory. This makes it a one-file directory rather than a genuine shared helpers zone. Moving crash-detector-test-support.ts into packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ alongside crash-detector.test.ts would eliminate the singleton directory and keep all crash zone test artifacts co-located.
- crash-detector-test-support.ts exports only constants (keys and thresholds) and has exactly one consumer: crash-detector.test.ts. A file with one consumer that exists solely to keep test constants out of the production file is a valid pattern, but co-locating it with its sole consumer (rather than a separate helpers/ directory) is the conventional placement and avoids introducing a singleton directory.
- Move packages/web/tests/helpers/crash-detector-test-support.ts into packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ alongside crash-detector.test.ts to eliminate the singleton helpers/ directory. The file has exactly one consumer (crash-detector.test.ts), so there is no sharing benefit from a separate directory.
- [call graph] 91 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 1 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
